data.content:
Plan for foolery-c257 — drop multi-key fallbacks in beat-table-metadata.tsx.

Scope verified: beat-table-metadata.tsx's exported readers are consumed only by capsuleMeta (called on handoff capsules) and renderedHandoffCapsules (which reads handoff_capsules arrays). Per ~/knots/docs/leases.md, handoff capsules stamp ONLY `agentname`, `model`, `version` (and `username`). Steps stamp `agent_name`/`agent_model`/`agent_version` — but this file does not read steps, so step keys do not belong here at all.

Refactor:
1. resolveAgentName/Model/Version/Username/Datetime currently try [agentname, agentName, agent_name] etc. Drop the alternates. For handoff capsules, the canonical key for agent name is `agentname` (one constant), for model is `model`, for version is `version`. No camelCase, no snake_case alternates.
2. Drop the `agent`/`metadata`/`actor` nested-object lookups for agent fields — capsules don't nest agent_info under those keys in Knots' stamping. Keep the top-level read only. (Username may still legitimately come from a nested `user` object in legacy fixtures — keep that path narrowly until tests prove otherwise; if the legacy fixtures don't exist in tests, drop those too.)
3. Remove imports/keys that become unused (e.g. helper-function `agent` / `actor` parameters where they're no longer consulted).
4. Update beat-table-summary.test.ts only if it asserts the old fallback behavior — current test asserts label ordering, not key fallbacks, so no change expected.
5. Verify with grep: `grep -n '"agentName"\|"agentname"\|"agent_name"' src/components/beat-table-metadata.tsx` shows exactly one `"agentname"` constant and zero of the other two.

Risk: low. The reader is a pure function over a passed entry. No callers depend on specific fallback shapes. Sibling knots foolery-988b (agent-identity.ts) and foolery-96ba (agent-history-utils.tsx) own untouched files.

Test strategy: existing `beat-table-summary.test.ts` and `beat-metadata-details.test.tsx` cover render flow. Will run lint + tsc + test + build. No new tests required for this knot — the contract enforcement is at the read site, and downstream renderers don't care about the omission of optional metadata.
